title: Varieties of Markoff Type: Arithmetic, Combinatorics, Dynamics
speaker: Alexander Gamburd
affiliation: City University of New York; Member, School of Mathematics
abstract: The Markoff equation x2+y2+z2=3xyz, which arose in his spectacular thesis (1879), is ubiquitous in a tremendous variety of contexts. After reviewing some of these, we will discuss (briefly) asymptotics of integer points, and (in some detail) recent progress towards establishing forms of strong approximation on varieties of Markoff type, as well as ensuing implications, diophantine and dynamical.

proof_stages.label: Classical Markoff equation and nonlinear orbit
proof_stages.summary: The talk introduces the Markoff equation, its positive integer triples and Markoff numbers, and the Vieta involutions whose nonlinear group orbit from the root solution organizes all Markoff triples.

proof_stages.label: Frobenius-Fricke background for Markoff type
proof_stages.summary: The Markoff equation is placed in its early generalizations, Frobenius's questions, the uniqueness conjecture, and the Fricke trace identity for SL_2 character varieties.

proof_stages.label: Counting and sparsity of integer points
proof_stages.summary: The speaker formulates counting Markoff numbers as counting integer points in growing balls and compares the classical log-squared asymptotic with higher-dimensional Markoff-Hurwitz counting exponents.

proof_stages.label: Square-free motivation and affine-linear contrast
proof_stages.summary: The question of infinitely many square-free Markoff numbers leads to reductions modulo primes and desired expansion, while comparison with affine-linear thin-group settings explains why the nonlinear Markoff action is harder but still amenable to proving that almost all Markoff numbers are composite.

proof_stages.label: Markoff graphs and approximation conjectures
proof_stages.summary: Markoff graphs are formally defined by reducing the Markoff surface modulo p, deleting the origin, and joining points by Vieta moves, with connectivity posed as strong approximation and expansion as super-strong approximation.

proof_stages.label: Product-replacement and character-variety obstruction
proof_stages.summary: The Markoff graph conjecture is related to product-replacement graphs for finite groups: higher-rank replacement graphs have connectivity/expansion results, while the k=2 SL_2 case is obstructed exactly by the Fricke-Markoff invariant.

proof_stages.label: Finite-prime theorem package
proof_stages.summary: The main results assert a giant Markoff component, a very small exceptional set of primes for strong approximation, Chen's divisibility of every finite orbit size by p, and lifting from p to prime powers, which combine to give connectivity/transitivity for all sufficiently large primes.

proof_stages.label: P-adic minimality and proof inputs
proof_stages.summary: The finite connectivity theorem is reinterpreted dynamically as minimality of the integral mapping-class-group action on p-adic Markoff varieties, with the proof using conic-section rotations, modular hyperbolicity/flattening in SL_2, and number-theoretic input.

examples.name: Golden and silver ratio sharpness cases
examples.note: The golden ratio and the quadratic irrational 1+sqrt(2) are cited as the extremal cases showing the sharpness of the first classical Markoff-type Diophantine approximation constants.
examples.engagement_reason: he states that the golden ratio and 1+sqrt(2) give the sharp cases for the approximation constants, without deriving or checking the sharpness on them

passes.reason: they realize the optimal Hurwitz/Markoff approximation constants, so they are extremal sharpness cases

examples.name: Integral Apollonian circle packing
examples.note: Descartes quadruples of circle curvatures are used as a parallel affine-linear setting where Vieta-type moves are linear and the thin-group sieve gives much stronger square-free and almost-prime results.
examples.engagement_reason: he sets up the Descartes equation for integral Apollonian packings, derives the Vieta/inversion move, and describes the orbit construction and consequences for curvatures

passes.reason: an integral Apollonian packing is a standard infinite Descartes-orbit thin-group example used for comparison

examples.name: Product-replacement graphs for SL_2(F_p)
examples.note: The k=2 product-replacement/character-variety case for SL_2(F_p) turns the Fricke identity into the Markoff obstruction, visibly motivating the conjecture that this is the only connectivity obstruction.
examples.engagement_reason: he defines the product-replacement graph via generator tuples and Nielsen moves and then discusses the SL_2(F_p) connectivity/obstruction cases
